articles.title: USTR Greer: We intend to stick to the China deal we had before
articles.snippet: I expect China tariff level to remain in placeWe don't intend to escalate tariffs on ChinaTrade law on discrimination against US could be helpful in some situationsNew trade investigations will look at excess industrial capacity, forced labour in supply chainsTariffs will go up to 15% for some countriesThere will be hearings and consultations with trading partnersHave been in touch with trade partners to explain how replacement tariffs go along with trade dealsU.S. Trade Representative Jamieson

articles.title: SOTU Trump: Says tariffs stay despite Supreme Court loss, pivots to new global levy
articles.snippet: Trump is reframing the Supreme Court tariff loss as a detour, not a derailment, as his team shifts the broad tariff base to Section 122 while keeping key sector and China-related tariffs intact.Summary:Trump addressed tariffs during his SOTU addressSupreme Court ruled IEEPA doesn’t authorise tariffs (Feb 20, 2026) White House pivoted to temporary global tariffs under Section 122 Rate set at 10% for 150 days; administration working toward 15% Many other tariffs (Section 232/301) remain intact Eco

articles.title: Copper: Chinese demand lifts prices – ING
articles.snippet: ING analysts Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey report that LME copper has moved back above $13,000/t as Chinese buyers return from Lunar New Year, with Yangshan premiums hitting a two‑month high.

articles.title: How Communists Conquered China
articles.snippet: The evidence tells a different story than you’ll find in the party's triumphant propaganda.

articles.title: Digital ecosystem makes China travels a breeze for Malaysians
articles.snippet: PETALING JAYA: For many Malaysians, the main draw of travelling to China is no longer just about the Great Wall or the scenic landscapes – it is the sheer convenience of stepping into a cashless society. Read full story

articles.title: China: Two Sessions set 2026 growth path – TD Securities
articles.snippet: TD Securities analysts expect Premier Li to unveil a 4.5–5.0% GDP target range for 2026 at the Two Sessions, alongside a broad budget deficit near 9% of GDP. Policymakers are seen prioritizing domestic demand, with continued targeted consumer stimulus.

combined_text:
[0.33] USTR Greer: We intend to stick to the China deal we had before
I expect China tariff level to remain in placeWe don't intend to escalate tariffs on ChinaTrade law on discrimination against US could be helpful in some situationsNew trade investigations will look at excess industrial capacity, forced labour in supply chainsTariffs will go up to 15% for some countriesThere will be hearings and consultations with trading partnersHave been in touch with trade partners to explain how replacement tariffs go along with trade dealsU.S. Trade Representative Jamieson 

[0.33] SOTU Trump: Says tariffs stay despite Supreme Court loss, pivots to new global levy
Trump is reframing the Supreme Court tariff loss as a detour, not a derailment, as his team shifts the broad tariff base to Section 122 while keeping key sector and China-related tariffs intact.Summary:Trump addressed tariffs during his SOTU addressSupreme Court ruled IEEPA doesn’t authorise tariffs (Feb 20, 2026) White House pivoted to temporary global tariffs under Section 122 Rate set at 10% for 150 days; administration working toward 15% Many other tariffs (Section 232/301) remain intact Eco

[0.33] Copper: Chinese demand lifts prices – ING
ING analysts Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey report that LME copper has moved back above $13,000/t as Chinese buyers return from Lunar New Year, with Yangshan premiums hitting a two‑month high.

[0.33] How Communists Conquered China
The evidence tells a different story than you’ll find in the party's triumphant propaganda.

[0.33] Digital ecosystem makes China travels a breeze for Malaysians
PETALING JAYA: For many Malaysians, the main draw of travelling to China is no longer just about the Great Wall or the scenic landscapes – it is the sheer convenience of stepping into a cashless society. Read full story

[0.33] China: Two Sessions set 2026 growth path – TD Securities
TD Securities analysts expect Premier Li to unveil a 4.5–5.0% GDP target range for 2026 at the Two Sessions, alongside a broad budget deficit near 9% of GDP. Policymakers are seen prioritizing domestic demand, with continued targeted consumer stimulus.
